creator: roam@orbitel.bg
text:
On Tue, Jul 03, 2001 at 01:43:07AM -0400, Pete Fritchman wrote:
> 
> >Number:         28661
> >Category:       ports
> >Synopsis:       New port: sysutils/service-config
> >Originator:     Pete Fritchman
> >Release:        FreeBSD 4.3-STABLE i386

OK, this looks great, and quite useful, too :)
I have a couple of suggestions for improvement, but I guess I'd
better submit them to the authors.

In the meantime, what do you think of the attached patch, which
makes this port honor CC and CFLAGS?

And.. might seem a minor matter, but.. I could not find any
licensing information at www.superscript.com.  If the software
there is derived from DJB's code (as it seems to be), then
it might be subject to http://cr.yp.to/softwarelaw.html and
the DJB's "license" (or lack thereof), which prohibits distribution
of modified software in binary form.  In that case, this port
would need a NO_PACKAGE directive.

Other than that, this looks fine :)

G'luck,
Peter

-- 
If I had finished this sentence,

diff -urN service-config/Makefile service-config-r1/Makefile
--- service-config/Makefile	Fri Jul  6 17:49:56 2001
+++ service-config-r1/Makefile	Fri Jul  6 17:54:31 2001
@@ -18,6 +18,8 @@
 INSTALL_TARGET=	setup check
 
 post-patch:
+	@${ECHO} "${CC} ${CFLAGS}" > ${WRKSRC}/conf-cc
+	@${ECHO} "${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${STRIP}" > ${WRKSRC}/conf-ld
 	@${PERL} -pi -e 's!/usr/local!${PREFIX}!g' ${WRKSRC}/conf-home
 
 .include <bsd.port.mk>
